test.notes: All tests pass including race detector, new test cases for critical fixes validated

typecheck.notes: Build passes for all platforms (docker unavailable but Go builds succeeded)

lint.notes: 88 dupl (pre-existing code duplication), 18 staticcheck (minor style). These are pre-existing issues not introduced by critical fixes.

appliedUpdates.description: Fixed compilation error in reactions.go: changed ReactionType{} to ReactionTypeEmoji{} for gotgbot v2 compatibility
Fixed test compilation in admin_test.go: changed *gotgbot.ChatMember to gotgbot.ChatMember (interface, not pointer to interface)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document gotgbot v2 interface patterns: ChatMember is an interface, not a struct. Use 'var x gotgbot.ChatMember' not '*gotgbot.ChatMember'. ReactionType is also an interface - use ReactionTypeEmoji for emoji reactions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: admin_test.go and reactions.go both had type errors with gotgbot v2 interfaces. Multiple workers encountered this issue.

sharedStateObservations.observation: CLAUDE.md error handling pattern is consistently followed: log.Error(err) followed by return err.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All three critical fixes follow this pattern: admin.go, blacklists.go

sharedStateObservations.observation: Goroutine patterns consistently use defer error_handling.RecoverFromPanic(context, func) at start.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: blacklists.go:128 and similar locations across codebase

sharedStateObservations.observation: Cache invalidation occurs after successful operations, never on error path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: blacklists_db.go:28 - deleteCache called after CreateRecord succeeds

sharedStateObservations.observation: In gotgbot v2, ChatMember is an interface type. Nil interface check (userMember == nil) is the correct pattern.
Telegram user IDs can exceed 32-bit range. Use strconv.ParseInt(str, 10, 64) for all user ID parsing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: captcha.go lines 631, 749, 1333, 1571 all use ParseInt with 64-bit

sharedStateObservations.observation: bugfix-worker skill procedure was followed correctly by all workers: locate bug with Grep, implement minimal changes, add test coverage, verify with go test.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All three critical fix transcripts show proper skill adherence

featureDetails.summary: Nil pointer dereference fix in admin.go demote() is correctly implemented. Nil check after GetMember() prevents panic when calling MergeChatMember().
Fire-and-forget goroutines now properly handle errors and have panic recovery. All 4 goroutines updated with error_handling.RecoverFromPanic().
All strconv.Atoi calls in captcha.go replaced with strconv.ParseInt(..., 10, 64). Test case covers edge cases including MaxInt64 and negative channel IDs.
